corrections.text: The article attributes the phrase 'hybrid intrusion model, in which ransomware is leveraged not as an end goal but as a mechanism for concealment' and the observation that 'the inclusion of extortion and negotiation elements could serve to focus defensive efforts on immediate impact' to SecurityWeek. These characterizations originate from Rapid7's own report; SecurityWeek was quoting Rapid7, not independently characterizing the campaign. The correct attribution for these phrases is Rapid7.

The article states that BleepingComputer 'notes the group previously deployed Thanos ransomware in September 2020 and Qilin ransomware in October 2025 against an Israeli organization.' The BleepingComputer source covers only the Qilin (late 2025) reference. The Thanos ransomware September 2020 claim is sourced from The Hacker News, not BleepingComputer.
